channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
item.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from Silverfor...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
item.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in que...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Robinhood account creation flaw abused to send phishing emails
item.description: Online trading platform Robinhood's account creation process was exploited by threat actors to inject phishing messages into legitimate emails, tricking users into believing their accounts had suspici...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GlassWorm malware attacks return via 73 OpenVSX "sleeper" extensions
item.description: A new wave of the Glassworm campaign is targeting the OpenVSX ecosystem with 73 "sleeper" extensions that turn malicious after an update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
item.description: A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom "Snow" malware in a multipronged campaign....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Canada arrests three for operating “SMS blaster” device in Toronto
item.description: Canadian authorities have arrested three men for operating an "SMS blaster" device that pretends to be a cellular tower to send phishing texts to nearby phones. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Alleged Silk Typhoon hacker extradited to US for cyberespionage
item.description: A Chinese national accused of carrying out cyberespionage operations for China's intelligence services has been extradited from Italy to the United States to face criminal charges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FTC: Americans lost over $2.1 billion to social media scams in 2025
item.description: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) warned of a massive increase in losses from social media scams since 2020, exceeding $2.1 billion in 2025. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Unpatched 'PhantomRPC' Flaw in Windows Enables Privilege Escalation
item.description: A researcher discovered five different exploit paths that stem from an architectural weakness in how Windows' Remote Procedure Call (RPC) mechanism handles connections to unavailable services....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: PyPI package with 1.1M monthly downloads hacked to push infostealer
item.description: An attacker pushed a malicious version of the popular elementary-data package Python Package Index (PyPI) to steal sensitive developer data and cryptocurrency wallets. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Home security giant ADT data breach affects 5.5 million people
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion group stole the personal information of 5.5 million individuals after breaching the systems of home security giant ADT earlier this month, according to data breach notificat...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Spotting cyberattacks before they begin
item.description: On Thursday, April 30 at 2:00 PM ET, BleepingComputer will host a live webinar with threat intelligence company Flare and threat intelligence researcher Tammy Harper, exploring how security teams can ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web. "Based ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Medtronic confirms breach after hackers claim 9 million records theft
item.description: Medical device giant Medtronic disclosed last week that hackers breached its network and accessed data in "certain corporate IT systems." [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
item.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed how e...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 20-Year-Old Malware Rewrites History of Cyber Sabotage
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a malware framework dubbed "fast16" that predates Stuxnet by five years....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Money launderer linked to $230M crypto heist gets 70 months in prison
item.description: ​22-year-old Evan Tangeman of Newport Beach, California, was sentenced to 70 months in prison for laundering funds stolen in a massive $230 million cryptocurrency heist. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Deepfake Voice Attacks are Outpacing Defenses: What Security Leaders Should Know
item.description: Three seconds of audio is all it takes to clone a voice for fraud. Adaptive Security shows how deepfake calls trick employees into sending real money—and why most defenses don't catch them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Parsing Agentic Offensive Security's Existential Threat
item.description: Some fear frontier LLMs like Claude Mythos and Anthropic's GPT-5.5 will lead to cybersecurity annihilation. Ari Herbert-Voss notes this could be an opportunity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft says Outlook.com outage is causing sign‑in failures
item.description: Microsoft is investigating an ongoing Outlook.com outage that is causing intermittent signing issues and preventing customers from accessing their mailboxes. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mythos Changed the Math on Vulnerability Discovery. Most Teams Aren't Ready for the Remediation Side
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview has dominated security discussions since its April 7 announcement. Early reporting describes a powerful cybersecurity-focused AI system capable of identifying vulnera...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group called PhantomCore has been attributed to attacks actively targeting servers running TrueConf video conferencing software in Russia since September 2025. That's accord...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Researchers Uncover 73 Fake VS Code Extensions Delivering GlassWorm v2 Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged dozens of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions on the Open VSX repository that are linked to a persistent information-stealing campaign dubbed Glass...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fake CAPTCHA IRSF Scam and 120 Keitaro Campaigns Drive Global SMS, Crypto Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a telecommunications fraud campaign that uses fake CAPTCHA verification tricks to dupe unsuspecting users into sending international text messages t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: American utility firm Itron discloses breach of internal IT network
item.description: Itron, Inc. has disclosed, via an 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party accessed certain internal systems. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft rolls out revamped Windows Insider Program
item.description: Microsoft says it's rolling out a revamped Windows Insider Program experience as part of the broader plans to address performance and reliability concerns affecting Windows 11. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Threat actor uses Microsoft Teams to deploy new “Snow” malware
item.description: A threat group tracked as UNC6692 uses social engineering to deploy a new, custom malware suite named 'Snow' which includes a browser extension, a tunneler, and a backdoor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment centrif...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets May 2026 Federal Deadline
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added four vulnerabilities impacting SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server, and D-Link DIR-823X series routers to its Known ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Helping Romance Scam Victims Requires a Proactive, Empathic Approach
item.description: People targeted by confidence schemes find getting help is a lonely road. Experts want law enforcement, financial, and government institutions to work together and protect them....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
